items.body:
🔴 **Design Concern** — `review/arm-alertsmanagement-node.api.md:491–495`
```ts
export enum KnownTimeRange {
    _1D = "1d",
    _1H = "1h",
    _30D = "30d",
    _7D = "7d"
}
```
Enum members `_1D`, `_1H`, `_30D`, `_7D` carry leading-underscore prefixes because they start with a digit, which is invalid as an identifier. This indicates poor naming on the spec side.

**Fix:** Prepare a PR to use `@clientName` in the spec repo's TypeSpec to give these members proper names (e.g., `OneHour`, `OneDay`, `SevenDays`, `ThirtyDays`), then trigger SDK regeneration.

2 design concerns were found in the public API surface of this new `@azure/arm-alertsmanagement` package. CI is currently failing on build/analyze/test stages, which should be investigated separately. Both findings below are spec-side naming issues that require `@clientName` fixes in the TypeSpec spec repo followed by SDK regeneration.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37633,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/alertsmanagement/arm-alertsmanagement/review/arm-alertsmanagement-node.api.md","line":495,"issueType":"design","category":"naming","description":"KnownTimeRange enum members _1D, _1H, _30D, _7D have leading-underscore prefixes due to digit-starting names; use `@clientName` in TypeSpec for proper names"},{"file":"sdk/alertsmanagement/arm-alertsmanagement/review/arm-alertsmanagement-node.api.md","line":618,"issueType":"design","category":"naming","description":"ProxyResource_1 exported alongside ProxyResource indicates a duplicated model name collision resolved via _N suffix; use `@clientName` to disambiguate in TypeSpec"}]}
```

</details>
